dateline: Tampa, Florida — Rest Day Ahead of Night 3; Rolling Stone and Tampa Bay Times Publish Rave Reviews of ARIRANG Tour Opener
heroHeadline: Day 4,701: Media Raves as BTS Rests Ahead of Tampa Night 3 — 'Magic Shop' Returns for First Time Since 2019
heroSubtitle: April 27, 2026 marks Day 4,701 since BTS's June 13, 2013 debut — a scheduled rest day between Tampa Night 2 (April 26) and the final Tampa Night 3 (April 28) at Raymond James Stadium. Rolling Stone published a review of the North America opener praising the emotional return of 'Magic Shop,' performed for the first time since 2019, while the Tampa Bay Times highlighted the Beatlemania-scale fan atmosphere, RM's seated-but-spirited performance, and a setlist bridging ARIRANG material with classic BTS anthems. Billboard chart data shows three additional ARIRANG tracks ('Hooligan' No. 90, '2.0' No. 88, 'Body to Body' No. 69) charting on the Hot 100 alongside 'SWIM' (No. 10), reflecting the album's sustained momentum in week 5. The ARIRANG World Tour's confirmed 31-show North American run — spanning 12 cities including El Paso, Mexico City, and New York — continues to dominate US media coverage.
footerNote: Data sourced from HYBE, Billboard, Hanteo Chart, Gaon Chart, Melon, Spotify, Netflix, Guinness World Records, IFPI, Korea Times, Korea Herald, Yonhap, Bloomberg, Variety, AP News, UPI, Starnews Korea, Rolling Stone, Tampa Bay Times, PBS NewsHour, Euronews, City of Tampa, Business Observer Florida, WTSP Tampa Bay, FOX 13 Tampa Bay, Tampa Bay Times, Bay News 9, ArtThreat, Soompi, IBTimes AU, Pannchoa, and Recording Academy.
